div.h2: Error Recovery Strategy: Skip-One-and-Continue vs Halt-on-First

g.text: WHY IT MATTERS — A File with 5 Bugs

div.p: Historical context: Early GCC halted on the first fatal error — developers discovered bugs one at a time. Clang was designed from day one to recover and collect all diagnostics in one pass. Rust's rustc follows Clang's philosophy and adds suggested fixes per error. Your scanner implements the Clang model: _scan_token() appends ERROR and returns normally to scan_tokens(), which calls _begin_token() and continues. Zero state to unwind — lexer recovery is free; parser recovery is hard.
